cot_content:
Okay, I need to figure out how to lock a tape measure. The options are A and B. Let me think.

A typical tape measure has a locking mechanism so you can hold it at a specific length. Usually, there's a small tab or button near the end of the tape. To lock it, you probably have to engage that mechanism. 

If I recall from using a tape measure, the locking tab is usually on the front part of the case. When you extend the tape to the desired length, you need to either push or pull the tab to lock it in place. 

If you pull the tab up, that might disengage the lock, allowing the tape to retract. Conversely, if you push the tab down, it would engage the lock, keeping the tape at the set length. 

So the correct action is likely to press the front locking tab down to lock the tape measure at the desired length. That would correspond to option B.
label: majority
